steps.title: Identify the temporal flow
steps.content: The word “once” at the start of the second sentence marks what comes next in the electoral process—after voters cast votes for parties, those votes are counted and seats are distributed.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
”Then” establishes this temporal sequence: voters first vote for parties, then votes are counted and seats are distributed accordingly.

Conversely and However suggest unwarranted contrast, while In other words suggests the second clause restates rather than extends the first.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
